Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A sum of Rs 90,000 is to be distributed amongst NOT more than 18 students as prize amount in denomination of Rs 500, Rs 2500, Rs 12,500 and Rs 62,500 such that there is at least one student getting a prize of each denomination and each student gets exactly one prize. The entire amount has to be distributed. In how many ways this amount can be distributed?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
We will start with one student each being recipient of one prize value. This implies that
62500 + 12500 + 2500 + 500 = Rs 78000 has been used up leaving a sum of Rs 12,000 to be distributed
among NOT more than 18 – 4 = 14 students. Since the amount remaining is less than Rs 12,500, this
amount has to be distributed as prizes of denomination Rs 2,500 and/or Rs 500 only.
We can start with four students receiving Rs 500 each and the remaining Rs 10,000 can be given to
four more students @ Rs 2,500 leading to total number of 12 students receiving the prizes.
Alternately, we can have 4 + 5 = 9 students receiving Rs 500 each and the remaining Rs 7,500 can
be given to three more students @ Rs 2,500 leading to total number of 16 students receiving the
prizes.
Any more options are ruled out since we have to increase in steps of 5 – 1 = 4 students only which
means that the next option will lead to 20 students getting the prize money which is not possible
since there are only 18 students in all.

Q15 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A process on Host C has a UDP socket bound to port 6789. Hosts A and B each send a UDP segment to Host C with destination port = 6789.
Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• Both UDP segments from Host A and Host B go to the same socket at Host C because UDP
demultiplexing is based solely on the destination port number. So, option (a) is correct.
• UDP does not consider the source port or source IP when delivering to a socket. Only the
destination port (6789) matters. So, option (b) is correct.
• The application at Host C can see:
(i) Source IP address (different for Host A and Host B)
(ii) Source port number
These values let Host C identify which host sent the packet. So, option (c) is correct.
• UDP does not auto-create separate sockets based on. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q20 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

What is the highest normal form of the relation R(A, B, C, D, E, F) having functional dependency set at

F = {A → BC, C → AD, E → ABC, F → CD, CD → BEF, AB → D}

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
If we find the candidate keys here we can clearly see that (A, C, E, F) are candidate keys. Hence
CD and AB are also superkeys. So all the FD’s will be like SUPERKEY → ATTRIBUTE.
That’s the only condition for BCNF.
Hence it is in BCNF normal form.

Q21 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following declaration of struct:
struct gate {
char m[20];
char * e;
struct gate * ptr;
} x[2] = {"GATE", "2026", x + 1, "MADEEASY", "COACHING", x}, *ptr = x;
What will be the output of the following print statement?
printf("%s", ptr ++ → ptr → e++);

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
ptr++ : x[0]
x[0] → ptr : x[1]
x[1] → e: COACHING

Q22 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

What is the language that is recognized by the PDA (Push Down Automata) given below:

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The PDA for this language works by first reading and pushing the a’s. When the a’s are done the
machine has all of them on the stack so that it can match them with either the b’s or the c’s.
Using its no-determinism, the PDA can guess whether to match the a’s with the b’s or with the c’s.

Q23 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Let G is a cyclic group with generator ‘a’. Order of ‘a’ is 29. The number of subgroups G has?

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2
Any subgroup of G must have an order that divides 29 (By Lagrange’s Theorem).
Only, 1 and 29 divide. So, only two subgroups are possible. Those are the trivial subgroups itself.
Note: For any Group of prime order there are only two subgroups.

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following situations for three Process P1, P2 and P3.
[Assume R1, R2 and R3 are resources]
S1 : P1 is holding R1 but it is tied up in a busy wait loop repeatedly trying to access R2, which it
never gets because R2 is held by P2. P2 is also stuck in a busy wait loop repeatedly trying to
access R3, which it never gets because R3 is held by P3. P3 is also in a busy wait loop repeatedly trying to access R1.
S2 : P1 is holding R1 and issued a blocking call for R2, which is held by P2. P2 issued a blocking call for R3.
Which is held by P3, P3 issued a blocking call for R1.
Which of the following is correct for above situations?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Situation S1 corresponds to livelock and situation S2 corresponds to deadlock.

Q29 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the relation:
Exam(eid, marks)
Assume that marks take not null integers only. Also, marks takes distinct values and the number
of tuples are odd.
Select marks from Exam E where (select Count(*) from Exam where marks < E.marks) = (select
Count(*) from Exam where marks > E.marks)
What does the above SQL query print?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
First subquery is counting marks which are less than E.marks, second one is counting which are
greater than E.marks. If both of these are equal, then it means that this value of E.marks lies
exactly in the middle. It is given that the number of tuples is odd, so there will be exactly one
middle. And the value of the marks is unique. So, we will get the median.

Q31 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The symmetric difference of language L and M which we will denote SD(L, M) is set of strings that are exactly in one of L and M but not both.
Let L = 0*1* and M = 1*0*
Which of the following is correct about symmetric difference of L and M.

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• SD(L, M) will have strings upto length 3 are 01, 10, 001, 011, 100, 110.
These are total 6 strings.
• Option (d) is also true, choose strings from 1 which are not in M or choose strings from M
which are not in L.

Q32 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In an IPv4 packet, which statement is TRUE about the data carried by IP?

Correct answer: C, A, B
Solution

(c)
The IPv4 header contains a protocol field that tells which upper-layer protocol the payload belongs
to. IP can carry many kinds of data, such as TCP (6), UDP (17), ICMP (1), IGMP (2), ESP (50),
AH (51), etc.
So IP is not limited to only TCP/UDP.

Q33 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider 4 processes P, Q, R and S scheduled on a CPU as per Round-Robin algorithm with a time quantum of 8 units. The processes arrive in the order P, Q, R, S all at time t = 0. There is exactly one context switch from S to Q, one context switch from R to Q and exactly two context switches from Q to R. There is no context switch from S to P. Switching to a ready process after the termination of another process is also a context switch. Which of the following is NOT possible as
CPU burst time of these processes?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
Given that processes arrive in the order P, Q, R and S. Since there is no context switch from S to P.
P must complete within 1 time quantum. There are 2 context switches from Q to R and exactly one
context switch from R to Q.
Q take longer to finish as compared to R. There is exactly one context switch from S to Q.
S must finish in a single time quantum. The sequence of jobs is P Q R S Q R Q.
Hence option (b) is incorrect as Q = 15 is not possible.
Q must take atleast 2 × 8 + 1 = 17 time units.
